jobName: Senior Basel Business Analyst (Credit Risk Management)
jobDescription:
About Net2Source Inc.

Net2Source is an award-winning total workforce solutions company recognized by Staffing Industry Analysts for our accelerated growth of 300% in the last 3 years with over 5500+ employees globally. We believe in providing staffing solutions to address the current talent gap 'Right Talent, Right Time, Right Place, Right Price' and acting as a Career Coach to our consultants.

The Role

We are looking for a solution-oriented Basel Business Analyst for a large financial institution. You will be responsible for defining and implementing technology solutions, gathering business requirements, and developing functional specifications with a special emphasis on use cases.
jobRequirement:
• Experience: 6+ years of relevant technical and business work experience.
  • Domain Knowledge: Deep understanding of Basel related projects, Credit Risks, and Banking & Financial domain (Risk management, Finance, and Accounting).
  • Technical Skills: Proficiency in MS Excel and experience with Relational Databases (RDBMS).
  • SQL: Ability to write SQL statements (mostly SELECT) to analyze complex datasets.
  • Methodologies: Extensive experience with various System Development Lifecycle (SDLC) methodologies.
  • Soft Skills: Strong analytical, organization, and user training skills.
moreDetail:
Hire Type: 6+ Month Contract (Extendable).
Location: Washington, D.C.
Contact: Prashant Singh (Sr. Account Manager) - prashant.singh@net2source.com.
Company Culture: We promote an inclusive culture focused on work-life fit and well-being. Net2Source is an Equal Opportunity Employer.
Accolades: America's Most Honored Businesses (Top 10%), NJ Biz Fastest 50, INC 5000 (8 consecutive years).

jobDescription:
About Nelson Engineering & Salas O'Brien

At Salas O'Brien, we are 'Engineered for Impact'. As an employee-owned firm founded in 1975, we focus on designing lasting solutions for pressing global challenges across data centers, healthcare, clean energy, and high-rise buildings.

Role Summary

In this internship, you will obtain invaluable hands-on technical experience that transcends classroom learning. You will work alongside engineer mentors in the design team, taking ownership of project portions and learning the development of complete design packages.
jobRequirement:
• Education: Currently enrolled in an Electrical Engineering program at a four-year ABET accredited university.
  • Academic Standing: Preferred graduation date within 1 year (will consider those within 2 years).
  • Technical Skills: Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook) and a strong desire to acquire CAD drafting skills.
  • Core Competencies: Well-organized, effective time management, and the ability to apply engineering concepts to real-world challenges.
  • Other: Must have reliable transportation and a laptop with Windows OS for work use.
moreDetail:
Work Schedule: Part-time Intern (Minimum 12 hours - Maximum 18 hours per week).
Location: Merritt Island, FL.
Legal Requirement: Verification of US Citizenship is mandatory upon hire.
Culture: We are a people-first firm committed to an inclusive culture, helping team members build unique, high-impact careers.
EEO: Salas O'Brien provides equal opportunities regardless of race, age, or disability status.

jobDescription:
About SEL Infrastructure Defense

Schweitzer Engineering Laboratories (SEL) seeks a talented Lead Software Engineer for our Cybersecurity division. We perform fascinating and rewarding work within the power engineering community, focusing on protecting critical infrastructure.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead a high-performing team in conducting white and black box testing of devices and software for security vulnerabilities.
  • Specification, design, and implementation of firmware/software systems.
  • Collaborate with Hardware Engineers to understand complex hardware-software interactions.
  • Mentor junior engineers and lead the development of design and test documentation.
jobRequirement:
• Education: B.S. in Computer Science, Cybersecurity, Electrical Engineering, or a related field.
  • Experience: Typically 6+ years in Object-Oriented or Embedded Programming (C, C++, C#, Python).
  • Security Domain: Expertise in Network Communications (ICS Protocols), Digital Forensics, and Reverse Engineering.
  • Analytical Skills: Experience with industrial control systems and cyber analysis operations (NICCS ID: PR-VAM-001).
  • Soft Skills: Strong leadership, mentorship abilities, and a solution-oriented mindset.
moreDetail:
Salary Range: $91,000 - $112,000 per year.
Legal Requirement: United States Citizenship is strictly required.
Location: Pullman, WA (Unmatched quality of life with access to mountains and forests).
Benefits: 100% Employee-owned (ESOP), 401K, top-tier medical/dental, and 10 paid holidays.
Growth: Tuition assistance and engineering development programs provided.

jobDescription:
About Capgemini Engineering

Capgemini Engineering is a world leader in engineering and R&D services. We help clients accelerate their journey towards Intelligent Industry by harnessing the power of cloud, data, AI, and digital engineering.

The Role

We are looking for a strong Software Engineer to help an Enterprise Client design, architect, and write documentation for an entire system. You will be responsible for coding entire software solutions, proactively reviewing team contributions as a 'code owner', and ensuring high-quality production releases.
jobRequirement:
• Education: Computer Science degree or equivalent experience.
  • Experience: 4-6 years of professional software engineering experience (Microsoft stack preferred).
  • Backend Skills: 3+ years of intensive experience with C#, .NET Core, and .NET.
  • DevOps & CI/CD: 5+ years with tools like GitHub, Jenkins, and Spinnaker; 3+ years of experience in DevOps deployments.
  • Testing: Expertise in automating unit, integration, and end-to-end (E2E) testing solutions.
  • Operations: Ability to provide operational support for your code and prevent technical risks within the domain.
moreDetail:
Salary Range: $65,200 - $138,944 per year (Base).
Employment Type: Full-time Regular.
Perks: 401(k), Employee Share Ownership Plan, and comprehensive healthcare (including mental health).
Family Support: Paid parental leave, adoption assistance, surrogacy, and cryopreservation benefits.
Culture: Mentoring, coaching, and a strong focus on unleashing human energy through technology.

jobName: Senior Java Fullstack Developer (Spring Boot & Angular)
jobDescription:
About Navy Federal Credit Union (NFCU)

Join the world's largest credit union! We are looking for a Senior Developer to lead and support Navy Federal development efforts in compliance with NFCU standards, ensuring the security and integrity of our enterprise systems.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead the analysis, design, and development of cost-effective IT solutions aligned with corporate strategies.
  • Provide Subject Matter Expertise and mentorship in software coding best practices.
  • Manage the System Development Life-cycle (SDLC) and Agile/Scrum methodologies.
  • Lead the implementation of applications into production environments and prepare migration plans.
jobRequirement:
• Education: Master's + 2 years OR Bachelor's + 4 years of work experience in Java Development.
  • Backend Stack: Expertise in Spring Boot, Spring Batch, and Spring MVC.
  • Frontend Stack: Experience in Single Page Application (SPA) development using Angular 2+, Bootstrap/Material, and NodeJS.
  • Data Access: Hands-on experience with JPA/Hibernate and performance tuning using caching mechanisms.
  • Architecture: Solid knowledge of Microservices and RESTful services.
  • Testing: Experience with unit testing using JUnit and Mockito.
moreDetail:
Work Environment: Collaborative Agile/Scrum development team.
Technical Focus: High emphasis on performance tuning and data integrity.
Professional Growth: Opportunities for mentorship and leading large-scale enterprise business solutions.
Standards: All development must adhere to strict NFCU software policies and security controls.

jobName: Software Engineering Manager (Core Acquiring Front End)
jobDescription:
About Fiserv

Fiserv is a global leader in Fintech and payments, moving money and information in a way that moves the world. We process over 350 million payment transactions per day with a peak throughput of over 8,000 transactions per second.

The Role

As a Software Engineering Manager, you will join our Core Acquiring Front End group. You will be a hands-on engineer picking up stories and code reviews while balancing platform stability, resiliency, and modern engineering practices.

Key Responsibilities

  • Manage cross-functional engineering teams and drive technical investment prioritization.
  • End-to-end platform ownership: design, build, and support resilient services solutions.
  • Partner with business owners to define platform roadmaps and scope features.
  • Provide thought leadership across acquiring engines and partner effectively with DevOps/Production Support.
jobRequirement:
• Experience: 15+ years of software engineering experience using C and Java.
  • Leadership: 10+ years of experience leading high-performing software engineering teams.
  • Domain: 5+ years of experience in Financial Services or Payments Processing.
  • Communication: Ability to clearly communicate with executive leadership.
  • Preferred: Experience in AIX and building solutions in public cloud (AWS, Azure, GCP).
  • Education: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or equivalent work experience.
moreDetail:
Perks: Competitive salary, Fiserv 401(k) Savings Plan, and Employee Stock Purchase Plan.
Well-being: Unlimited Recharge & Refuel for qualifying associates, mental health programs, and free counseling via EAP.
Growth: Internal mobility, professional certifications, and training through 'Fuel Your Life' program.
Company Culture: FORTUNE 500 company, ranked as one of Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies.

jobDescription:
About OvaInnovations

We're reinventing the egg protein business! At OvaInnovations, we deliver high-quality egg protein for pet food, livestock, and aquaculture industries. We have undergone rapid growth and are looking for a dedicated team member to join our lean, active work environment.

The Role

Reporting to the Controller, the Senior Accountant is responsible for coordinating the design, testing, and implementation of complex manual or automated financial systems. You will have an immediate impact across the enterprise, supporting a diverse range of financial goals.
jobRequirement:
• Education: Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.
  • Certification: Certified Public Accountant (CPA) is strongly preferred.
  • Experience: 5-7 years of professional accounting experience (or 3 years if CPA certified). Experience in manufacturing or production environments is a plus.
  • Technical Skills: Thorough understanding of GAAP and auditing standards. Proficiency with accounting software (Acumatica experience is a bonus).
  • Soft Skills: Strong analytical, problem-solving, and organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple concurrent deadlines.
moreDetail:
Impact: Growth-oriented company with potential for rapid development.
Software: Familiarity with Slack, G-Suite, and Asana for project management.
Audit: Responsible for maintaining financial controls and assisting outside auditors with annual audits.
EEO: OvaInnovations is committed to equal employment opportunity and avoids artificial barriers to employment.

jobName: Sales Tax Accountant (Tax Compliance & Audit Support)
jobDescription:
About Farmer Brothers

At Farmer Brothers, we are dedicated to financial prosperity through strict adherence to legal tax requirements. We are seeking a Sales Tax Accountant to focus on tax compliance, including sales tax, property tax, and business licenses.

Key Responsibilities

  • Prepare and file sales & use tax returns on a monthly, quarterly, and annual basis using Vertex software.
  • Manage property tax returns and renew business licenses, including Tribal and Casino Vendor licenses.
  • Troubleshoot notices and respond to inquiries regarding tax-related payments and filings.
  • Conduct and document sales tax audit support and assist with various Tax Department projects.
jobRequirement:
• Education: Bachelor's degree in Accounting or Finance is required.
  • Certification: CPA license is highly preferred.
  • Experience: Minimum of 3+ years of tax accounting experience; public company experience is a plus.
  • Technical Skills: Advanced proficiency in MS Office and experience with Vertex tax system (preferred).
  • Soft Skills: Keen attention to detail, highly organized, and excellent problem-solving abilities to implement business solutions across departments.
moreDetail:
Environment: Enterprise-level tax management and public company reporting standards.
Legal Focus: Requires great knowledge of current laws, regulations, and tax requirements.
Specialization: Handling occupational permits with cities and Tribal authorities.
Commitment: Looking for a resourceful and innovative individual with a strong work ethic to meet competing deadlines.

jobName: Business Analyst (Digital Product Lifecycle & User Experience)
jobDescription:
About the Role

The Business Analyst will support cross-functional project teams focused on driving the end-to-end digital product lifecycle. The goal is to improve the overall online experience for residents and businesses within the Connecticut Digital Service.

Key Responsibilities

  • Perform detailed requirements analysis and evaluate business processes to uncover areas for improvement.
  • Prioritize requirements based on Product Roadmaps, business needs, and technical feasibility.
  • Stay up to date on the latest IT advancements to automate and modernize government systems.
  • Establish continuous improvement/delivery pipelines based on customer research and feedback.
jobRequirement:
• Requirements Analysis: At least 5+ years of experience in evaluating business processes and creating Use Cases.
  • Documentation: 3+ years of experience documenting User Flows, System Flows, User Stories, and Acceptance Criteria.
  • Testing (UAT): 2+ years of experience ensuring solutions meet business needs and performing User Acceptance Testing.
  • Digital Platforms: 2+ years working with enterprise-scale platforms (omni-channel service, CRM, or CMS).
  • Tools: Proficiency in JIRA, Confluence, Google Analytics, Git Repository, and Microsoft Teams.
moreDetail:
Duration: 12+ Month Contract.
Environment: Working with state-wide partners and government staff requiring high diplomacy and judgment.
Skills: Exceptional analytical thinking and the ability to influence stakeholders in complex situations.
Methodology: Iterative delivery and modernization of legacy systems.

jobDescription:
About the Job at Google

Google's software engineers develop next-generation technologies that change how billions of users connect and interact with information. Android is our open-source mobile OS powering over 3 billion devices worldwide. We are looking for engineers who bring fresh ideas from all areas, including distributed computing, large-scale system design, and UI design.

Key Responsibilities

  • Write product or system development code and design software solutions.
  • Manage project priorities, deadlines, and deliverables with technical expertise.
  • Review code developed by other engineers and provide feedback to ensure best practices (style, accuracy, testability).
  • Triage, debug, and resolve product or system issues by analyzing hardware, network, and service operations.
  • Lead design reviews with peers and stakeholders to decide amongst available technologies.
jobRequirement:
• Education: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or equivalent practical experience.
  • Android Development: 2+ years of experience in Android application development.
  • Core CS: 2+ years of experience with Data Structures and Algorithms in an industry setting.
  • Programming: Proficiency in one or more programming languages (Java, Kotlin, C++).
  • Preferred: Master's or PhD in CS; Experience with large-scale systems, visualization tools, and accessible technologies.
  • System Health: Proficiency in system diagnosis, resolution, and software test engineering.
moreDetail:
Total Compensation: Base Salary $133,000 - $194,000 + bonus + equity + benefits.
Preferred Locations: Seattle, WA or Mountain View, CA.
Equal Opportunity: Google is an affirmative action employer committed to equality regardless of race, religion, or orientation.
Environment: Opportunity to switch teams and projects as the business evolves.

jobDescription:
About OvaInnovations

OvaInnovations is reinventing the egg protein business, delivering high-quality value for the pet food, livestock, and aquaculture industries. We are a growth-oriented company with a lean, active work environment capable of incredible growth.

The Role

Reporting to the Controller, the Senior Accountant will be responsible for coordinating the design, testing, implementation, and review of complex manual or automated financial and accounting systems. This is a role where you can have an immediate impact across the enterprise.
jobRequirement:
• Experience: 5-7 years of professional accounting or finance experience (or 3 years with a valid CPA license).
  • Technical Skills: Thorough understanding of GAAP and auditing standards. Proficiency with accounting software (Acumatica experience is a plus).
  • Industry Background: Experience in a multi-facility production or manufacturing environment is highly preferred.
  • Project Management: Familiarity with office and organization tools such as Slack, G-Suite, and Asana.
  • Competencies: Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and a highly collaborative team-player mindset.
moreDetail:
Impact: Direct participation in system design alternatives and identifying potential improvements.
Audit Support: Provides outside auditors with assistance and maintains general ledger integrity.
Culture: We are 'good eggs' committed to equal employment opportunity and a barrier-free workplace.
Systems: Focus on maintaining internal controls and security of automated accounting applications.

jobDescription:
About Landis & Associates

We are a technology-forward, faith-based accounting firm that aims to inspire and empower our clients. We take our work seriously, but never ourselves, fostering an amazingly talented, passionate, and hardworking team culture.

What You Will Do

This is not just a job for number crunchers. You will consult with clients to help them make business decisions based on customized KPIs and advise on Tax Strategy to help them steward their resources well.

  • Perform Tax Preparation for 1040, 1065, and 1120S.
  • Resolve complex tax notices so clients can focus on their priorities.
  • Manage and review bookkeeping using QuickBooks Online (QBO).
jobRequirement:
• Experience: 3+ years of experience preparing complex, high-quality tax returns in a CPA firm.
  • Education/Certification: Currently holding or actively pursuing an EA (Enrolled Agent) or CPA license.
  • Technical Skills: Tech-savvy and excited about new technologies; proficiency in QuickBooks Online is a must.
  • Soft Skills: Exceptional problem-solver, detail-oriented ('persnickety'), and a service-focused 'people-person'.
  • Discipline: Ability to prioritize work and stay focused in a remote-capable environment, especially during a 55-hour work cap in tax season.
moreDetail:
Work Mode: Fully Remote (Note: Classified as Hybrid in system).
Schedule: Flexible schedule, including Half-day Fridays (Summer) and NO SUNDAYS.
Benefits: Health Insurance, 401k, and strong opportunities for growth.
Culture: Faith-based company with a Stewardship Program supporting local non-profits.
Pay Range: $45,000 - $65,000+ per year DOE.

jobName: Senior Software Engineer (Cloud Infrastructure Asset Management)
jobDescription:
About the Role at Oracle

Oracle is seeking a driven Senior Software Engineer to join a team of professionals revolutionizing the delivery of cloud data centers globally. You will build, test, and operate highly scalable solutions for the canonical asset management system of Oracle’s next-generation Cloud Infrastructure.

Key Responsibilities

  • Develop highly scalable solutions for global cloud data centers.
  • Dive deep into any part of the stack and low-level systems as a technical generalist.
  • Work in an Agile/DevOps environment to manage multiple projects and priorities.
  • Communicate complex technical information clearly to stakeholders across multiple global locations.
jobRequirement:
• Education: BS or MS degree in Computer Science or equivalent.
  • Experience: Minimum 6 years in software engineering, with 3+ years in Agile/DevOps environments.
  • Programming: 4+ years of Python development and 3+ years of Java development.
  • Technical Core: Strong knowledge of Microservices, Data Structures, Algorithms, and Distributed Systems.
  • Infrastructure: Solid understanding of Linux/Unix fundamentals, Relational Databases (RDBMS), and NoSQL systems.
  • APIs & Cloud: 3+ years of REST API development; experience with Docker, Kubernetes, and CI/CD tools (Jenkins, Ansible) is a plus.
moreDetail:
Legal Requirement: US Citizens able to obtain a security clearance.
Preferred Skills: Experience with React JS, observability tools, and data center operations.
Soft Skills: Excellent troubleshooting, performance tuning, and the ability to thrive in ambiguity.
Environment: Working with distributed teams across global time zones.

jobDescription:
About PacMar Technologies LLC

PacMar Technologies is a privately-owned defense contractor devoted to solving novel technological problems in Maritime Platform Design, Autonomy, and Data Science. You will join a talented group of Engineers and Innovators in our Honolulu office.

The Role

Reporting to the Senior Accountant, the Staff Accountant will receive, process, verify, and reconcile invoices to ensure timely payments. You will play a key role in maintaining the financial integrity of projects with national defense implications.
jobRequirement:
• Education: Bachelor’s degree with an emphasis in Accounting or Finance.
  • Core Accounting: Experience in maintaining Vendor Listings, gathering W-9 forms, and issuing 1099s at Year-End.
  • Expense Management: Skilled in reviewing employee reimbursement requests (travel, meals, lodging) and managing monthly Corporate Card expenses.
  • Technical Skills: Proficient in MS Office (Word, Excel). Experience with Job Costing and JAMIS PRIME software is highly desired.
  • Attributes: Extremely detail-oriented, ability to prioritize assignments, and strong interpersonal communication skills.
moreDetail:
Legal Requirement: Must be a U.S. Citizen or Permanent Resident due to Export Control purposes.
Location: Honolulu, Hawaii.
Compliance: Employment is contingent upon passing a background check and post-offer drug screen.
Environment: Team-oriented workspace with significant top-side potential for career development.

jobName: Senior Business Analyst (Product Engineering - Audit Data Analytics)
jobDescription:
About Crowe

Crowe is one of the largest public accounting, consulting, and technology firms in the US, routinely recognized as a Best Place to Work. Our Product Engineering practice builds reliable technology solutions across various industries, including financial services and healthcare.

The Role

We are seeking a Senior Business Analyst to join a Scrum team responsible for developing Ignite, a high-impact data analytics product for our Audit practice. You will lead requirements elicitation, story refinement, and act as a change agent for Agile process improvements.
jobRequirement:
• Experience: 5+ years of experience as a Business Analyst in a software delivery environment.
  • Agile Expertise: Proficient in Agile Scrum methodology; experience with Scaled Agile Framework (SAFe) and Product Owner/Scrum Master certifications is a plus.
  • Technical Skills: Strong understanding of SDLC, Product Development, and Data Driven/Data Heavy products.
  • Tools: Proficiency in Azure DevOps, SharePoint, Microsoft 365, and Power BI.
  • Leadership: Ability to lead backlog management, coordinate Agile ceremonies, and partner with Development Leads on Scrum Master responsibilities.
moreDetail:
Industry Focus: Professional services, Finance, or Accounting.
Product: Focused on the Ignite data analytics platform for Audit services.
Growth: Inclusive culture with dedicated Career Coaches to guide your aspirations.
Methodology: High emphasis on decomposition of large-scale epics and User Acceptance Testing (UAT) coordination.

jobDescription:
About Hallgate Management

Hallgate Management is a premier property management company dedicated to excellence, integrity, and continuous growth. We are committed to providing exceptional service to our residents and maintaining high-quality standards across our multifamily portfolio.

Position Overview

We are seeking a detail-oriented Multifamily Accountant to manage the financial aspects of our properties. You will ensure financial accuracy, regulatory compliance, and timely reporting while collaborating with property managers and leasing agents to optimize operations.
jobRequirement:
• Education: Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.
  • Experience: Minimum of 3 years of accounting experience, specifically focused on the multifamily real estate sector.
  • Technical Skills: Proficient in GAAP standards and experienced with property management/accounting software.
  • Financial Analysis: Ability to conduct ROI, cash flow, and profitability analysis for real estate assets.
  • Compliance: Knowledge of local, state, and federal tax regulations related to multifamily properties.
  • Soft Skills: Strong organizational, analytical, and problem-solving skills with the ability to work independently.
moreDetail:
Key Responsibilities: Preparing monthly financial statements, variance analysis, and monitoring actual performance against budget.
Operations: Overseeing accounts payable/receivable, rent collections, and reconciling tenant ledgers.
Benefits: Competitive salary with bonus structure, comprehensive health/dental/vision insurance, and retirement savings with company match.
Environment: A supportive, collaborative workplace with professional development opportunities.

jobDescription:
About Allvue Systems

Allvue is the leading provider of software solutions for the Private Capital and Credit markets. We help eliminate the boundaries between systems, information, and people. Working at Allvue means working with pioneers in the Fintech industry to build adaptable financial software solutions.

The Role

We are seeking an ambitious Revenue Specialist to join our team. You will be responsible for the preparation, posting, and reconciliation of revenue in accordance with ASC 606 and managing complex billing rules for software and professional services projects.
jobRequirement:
• Experience: Minimum of 5 years of relevant experience as a revenue or billing specialist, preferably within the Software or Fintech industries.
  • Technical Knowledge: Strong proficiency in ASC 606 revenue recognition standards and GAAP financial statements.
  • Software Skills: Experience with NetSuite and/or Salesforce is highly preferred; excellent knowledge of MS Office.
  • Contract Analysis: Ability to interpret complex contracts and Statements of Work (SOW) to create corresponding billing rules.
  • Attributes: Exceptional analytical aptitude, detail-oriented, and the ability to perform well under pressure in a fast-paced SaaS environment.
moreDetail:
What We Offer: Enterprise Udemy membership with access to thousands of courses, competitive pay, and year-end bonus potential.
Work-Life Balance: Flexible PTO, charitable donation matching, and numerous team-building activities.
Process Improvement: Opportunity to improve closing processes for a rapidly growing SaaS company and build scalable, technology-driven accounting operations.
Compliance: Assist in managing tax filings and internal controls for financial reporting.

jobName: Business Analyst (Lead II - Healthcare IT Solutions)
jobDescription:
About Advantasure & UST HealthProof

Advantasure champions innovative solutions for health plans, providing flexibility to adapt to changing regulatory environments. We leverage leading experts in government-sponsored health plans to offer solutions for administrative cost management, risk adjustment, and quality provider engagement.

The Role

As a Lead II Business Analyst, you will ensure every functional business consideration is identified before implementation. You will translate application functionality into architecture, manage the development life cycle, and partner with clients to formulate business process specifications.
jobRequirement:
• Experience: 4-8 years of experience as a Business Analyst specifically in Healthcare, Health Insurance, Medicare, or Commercial business lines.
  • Modeling Expertise: Strong knowledge of Business Models, Process Models, Data Models, Workflow Models, and Object Models.
  • Technical Documentation: Expertise in creating functional specifications, database/UI designs, Use Cases, and Statements of Work (SOW).
  • Tools & Methods: Proficiency in Visio for workflow diagrams and experience with various project management methodologies (Bi-modal).
  • Soft Skills: Exceptional presentation, negotiation, and analytical problem-solving skills in potentially chaotic environments.
moreDetail:
Work Mode: Remote (System Tag: Hybrid).
Compensation: Starting range $78,000 - $118,000 per year.
Benefits: 401K matching, healthcare, dental, vision, life, and disability insurance covered from Day 1.
Values: Driven by Humility, Humanity, and Integrity.
Focus: Championing innovative healthcare solutions and making a difference in the US healthcare system.

jobName: EDI Business Analyst (SAP Integration & Trading Partner Management)
jobDescription:
About the Role

Vaco Technology is seeking an EDI Business Analyst to ensure efficient electronic data interchange operations. You will be a key player in analyzing EDI transactions, collaborating with trading partners, and utilizing SAP systems to facilitate seamless communication and data exchange.

Key Responsibilities

  • Optimize EDI processes to ensure accuracy, compliance, and successful data transmission.
  • Lead the onboarding of new EDI trading partners by coordinating agreements, data mapping, and communication protocols.
  • Manage IDocs within SAP, serving as electronic data containers for information exchange.
  • Troubleshoot missing EDI orders using a methodical approach, liaising with internal teams and external partners.
jobRequirement:
• EDI Expertise: In-depth knowledge of EDI principles and standards, specifically EDI 204 (motor carrier load tendering).
  • SAP Proficiency: Skilled in SAP transactions relevant to EDI, including t-code WE02 (Display IDoc) and t-code WE20 (Partner Profile).
  • Technical Integration: Experience working with APIs, SQL, BizTalk, and SAP PI.
  • Data Analysis: Ability to utilize SAP table KNMT for order processing, tracking, and reporting.
  • Analytical Skills: Strong problem-solving mindset to resolve complex data transmission issues and validate outbound transactions.
moreDetail:
Position Type: Full-time Direct Hire.
Location: North Dallas metroplex (Hybrid - onsite/remote).
Compensation: Base salary up to $105,000 + performance bonus.
Expertise: Familiarity with VAN (Value Added Network) services for secure EDI communication.
Collaboration: Requires excellent communication skills to engage with diverse cross-functional teams and external partners.

jobDescription:
About Progressive Leasing

Progressive Leasing, a subsidiary of PROG Holdings (NYSE: PRG), is a leading provider of in-store and e-commerce lease-to-own solutions. We are an ever-evolving group of technologists and financial experts focused on streamlining processes for high-risk areas in the FinTech industry.

The Role

We are seeking a talented Senior Accountant to join our Accounting Department. You will take ownership of loan accounting, general ledger, and revenue recognition while working cross-functionally with other subsidiaries like Vive Financial and Four (a BNPL platform).
jobRequirement:
• Education: Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, or equivalent experience.
  • Experience: 2+ years in a public accounting firm or 4+ years in a large public corporation.
  • Technical Skills: High proficiency in Excel (Power Query, SQL, PivotTables) and experience with Microsoft Dynamics Great Plains or similar ERPs.
  • Industry Knowledge: Strong understanding of GAAP, revenue recognition, and SOX requirements. Experience with consumer loans or AR reserves is highly preferred.
  • Financial Operations: Expertise in month-end close, balance sheet reconciliations, and maintaining monthly accruals and reserves.
moreDetail:
Location: Draper, UT (Hybrid).
Benefits: Competitive salary, 401k match, stock purchase program, and tuition reimbursement.
Specialization: Focused on Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) platforms and high-traffic online transaction processing.
Culture: Dedicated to inclusive collaboration, innovation, and professional development.

jobName: Sales Tax Accountant (Tax Compliance & Audit Support)
jobDescription:
About Farmer Brothers

At Farmer Brothers, we are dedicated to financial prosperity through strict adherence to legal tax requirements. We are seeking a Sales Tax Accountant to focus on tax compliance, including sales tax, property tax, and business licenses. You will play a key role in recommending best tax processes that promote our company's financial growth.

Key Responsibilities

  • Prepare and file Sales & Use tax returns on a monthly, quarterly, and annual basis using Vertex software.
  • Manage property tax returns and renew business licenses, including specialized Tribal and Casino Vendor licenses.
  • Troubleshoot notices and respond to inquiries for all tax-related payments and filings.
  • Conduct and document Sales Tax Audit support and collaborate with other departments to implement business solutions.
jobRequirement:
• Education: Bachelor's degree in Accounting or Finance is required.
  • Certification: CPA license is highly preferred.
  • Experience: Minimum of 3+ years of tax accounting experience, preferably in a public company environment.
  • Technical Skills: Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office and experience utilizing the Vertex tax system (a plus).
  • Soft Skills: Keen attention to detail, highly organized, and the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously under strict deadlines.
  • Professionalism: Open and straightforward with a strong work ethic and the ability to engage with external auditors.
moreDetail:
Industry: Public company environment with a focus on enterprise system standards.
Legal Focus: Requires great knowledge of current laws, regulations, and tax requirements.
Impact: Strategic role in recommending tax processes that adhere to legal requirements while promoting financial prosperity.
Communication: Strong written and verbal skills are essential for cross-departmental collaboration.

jobDescription:
About the Opportunity

Alexander Technology Group is exclusively partnering with a local Food and Beverage Manufacturer currently in growth mode. We are seeking a direct-hire ERP Business Analyst to play a key role in their ongoing Dynamics 365 implementation.

The Role

You will join the organization mid-implementation, leveraging your strong functional background to bridge the gap between business needs and technical execution. This is a hands-on role where you will drive the 'Plan to Deliver' integrated business processes across manufacturing and supply chain departments.
jobRequirement:
• Experience: 5-9 years of experience as a Business Analyst, specifically within full lifecycle ERP implementations (Dynamics 365, SAP, Oracle, or NetSuite).
  • Domain Expertise: Deep knowledge of the 'Plan to Deliver' process, including demand planning, manufacturing, and supply chain management.
  • Financial Modules: Strong functional background in the Financial suite of modules within an ERP environment.
  • Technical Skills: Proficiency in SQL query experience, master data setup, data structures, and advanced data analysis using Excel or BI tools.
  • Industry Knowledge: Previous experience in Manufacturing, Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG), or the Food industry is highly preferred.
  • Soft Skills: Exceptional change management, project coordination, and the ability to diagram current processes to identify areas for improvement.
moreDetail:
Compensation: $80k - $120k + 10% Bonus (Salary displayed is converted estimate).
Work Mode: Hybrid - Requires 3 days onsite (Tue-Thur) in the Dedham/Westwood area.
Legal: Must be able to work in the US without sponsorship.
Education: Bachelor's degree in a related business or technical major is required.
